title: Limiting Submissions on a WorkForm
excerpt: Looking for input or ideas: After a requester submits an initial form, dependent on their selections, an automation will run to send a request specific URL to the individual. This link carries over information to the new…

posts.author: lizo (Liz Burnett)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looking for input or ideas: After a requester submits an initial form, dependent on their selections, an automation will run to send a request specific URL to the individual. This link carries over information to the new form from their initial submission. In some cases, a user with submit multiple/separate requests using this same link. Is there a way to prevent this in Monday? Link expiration?

We cannot turn off the “Submit multiple forms” in the WorkForms settings because most users have >1 request.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you provide some more detail? Where is the link coming from? Is it just in the template, is it in a columN?

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @lizo If you are open to 3rd party marketplace apps, you can do that with SuperForm. It’s a form like WorkForm that has the functionalities you described.

SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @lizo,

This is something FormFlow handles pretty well. It has built-in Submission Rules that give you granular control over resubmissions. You can allow or block resubmits per form/link, set exact limits, and show custom messages when the limit is hit. So it works independently of a global “submit multiple forms” toggle.

Theres also time window restrictions (only accept submissions during a certain period) and country restrictions if thats relevant for you.
